replay_env: GE007_DISABLE_LEVEL_INTRO=1 GE007_AUTO_ADD_ITEM=8 GE007_AUTO_ADD_ITEM_FRAME=30 GE007_AUTO_ADD_WEAPON_AMMO=8 GE007_AUTO_ADD_WEAPON_AMMO_AMOUNT=600 GE007_AUTO_ADD_WEAPON_AMMO_FRAME=30 GE007_AUTO_EQUIP_ITEM=8 GE007_AUTO_EQUIP_ITEM_FRAME=45
sim_hash_policy_note: Re-baselined 2026-07-23 for FID-0046 opaque-pool pointer canonicalization. Authentic ON remains distinct from legacy OFF; gameplay/input is unchanged.
note: AK47 (ITEM_AK47=8, full-auto machinegun) sustained-fire tape on the Dam (FID-0056 / P1d). Bond is given+equipped the AK47 with 600 rifle rounds (replay_env, deterministic state injection) and holds fire for 400 ticks (GE007_AUTO_FIRE=80:400 baked into the captured OSContPad stream). Unlike the PP7 tape (dam_combat_guard6) this reaches the full-auto machinegun case in src/game/gun.c (~line 18359-18372: fire_rate = bondwalkItemGetAutomaticFiringRate() then fireRateEffectiveAutoRate()), so it is the end-to-end gate for the FID-0056 fire-rate call-site that the ROM-free unit test (tests/test_fire_rate_authentic.c) cannot cover (P1d: the unit test re-implements the gate; a call-site revert stays green). sim_state_hash is the DEFAULT (Input.FireRateAuthentic=1, faithful N64 cadence) hash; fire_rate_authentic_off_hash is the pre-FID-0056 legacy-cadence hash (GE007_FIRE_RATE_AUTHENTIC=0), which is exactly what a gun.c call-site revert reproduces. ON != OFF proves the tape exercises the divisor: a call-site revert reddens this baseline. Deterministic: record==replay1==replay2==replay3 (byte-exact). Regenerate only via scratchpad/record_ak47_final.sh (canonical recipe; keep in sync with tools/fidelity/ak47_fire_rate_capture_smoke.sh). Guarded by tools/fidelity/ak47_fire_rate_capture_smoke.sh (ctest port_ak47_fire_rate_capture_smoke) + tools/fidelity/tape_regression.sh (via replay_env). Re-baselined 2026-07-11 (857329bd0ebe6c07→e228f3a765adc3eb): this Dam tape's window also holds a guard (chr=6, KF7/item=8, AutomaticFiringRate=3) in sustained line-of-sight full-auto at Bond; the recorded 857329bd0ebe6c07 was captured (commit 9e661b3) against a stale pre-FID-0066 ge007 binary (linked before the FID-0066 chrlv.c fix landed, despite the source tree already containing it), so it reflects a mixed state -- player fire-rate scaled (FID-0056), guard fire-rate NOT yet scaled (pre-FID-0066) -- that no longer exists once guards are correctly tick-scaled too (FID-0066, chrlv.c chrlvFireWeaponRelated). fire_rate_authentic_off_hash is UNCHANGED and still exactly reproduces the pre-FID-0056/pre-FID-0066 legacy cadence (verified identical on both a git-archive build of b289b60, the pre-merge-train tip, and the current tree). See docs/fidelity/derivations/FID-0066-npc-fire-rate.md and the P1a/Phase-B repair evidence for the full attribution (GE007_TRACE_GUARD_AUTOFIRE confirms chr=6 gate-fires 18x inside this tape's window at eff_rate=9). Re-baselined 2026-07-11 (e228f3a765adc3eb->8c1c6f13a440c0eb) for FID-0014: the faithful WAYMODE_MAGIC patrol semantics (default ON) freeze unseen patrolling/gopos guards and pad-warp them per retail chrTickBeams US 0x7F020EF0 / chrlvTravelTickMagic US 0x7F028600, so offscreen patroller positions (sim state) differ from the legacy creep-walk. GE007_NO_PATROL_MAGIC_FIX=1 reproduces the previous hash e228f3a765adc3eb byte-identically (verified on this tape). See docs/fidelity/derivations/FID-0014-patrol-magic.md. fire_rate_authentic_off_hash likewise re-based a950d75eb42017f2->2c93c8d798811fe2 (= fix-ON default + GE007_FIRE_RATE_AUTHENTIC=0; the FID-0066 opt-out contract is unchanged). Re-baselined 2026-07-12 (8c1c6f13a440c0eb->86f16b09082af5d6) for FID-0046: the effect-buffer zero-init (default ON, initexplosioncasing.c) zeroes g_ExplosionBuffer free-slot dead words (parts[].pos/size/rot, read only when frame>0) that this level's file-load left non-zero in the hashed pool region. Gameplay is byte-identical -- the words are dead memory the game never reads; screenshots are identical fix-ON vs fix-OFF. GE007_NO_EFFECT_BUF_ZERO_INIT=1 reproduces the prior hash 8c1c6f13a440c0eb byte-identically (opt-out A/B verified across 3 runs). See docs/fidelity/ledger/FID-0046.json. Re-baselined 2026-07-12 (86f16b09082af5d6->a12bbed88c612c91) for FID-0117: the root-motion seed flag in modelSetAnimFrame2WithChrStuff now reads rwdata->unk01 (offset 1, retail) instead of the port's erroneous rwdata->unk02 (offset 2). The flag is written to unk01 by modelSetAnimation2 and read at offset 1 by retail (ASM 7F0702EC: lb 1(v0), stored to sp+0x94==save_unk01); the port read the wrong adjacent byte, altering character root-motion accumulation on animation transitions. GE007_NO_ANIMFRAME2_ROOTFLAG_FIX=1 reproduces the prior hash 86f16b09082af5d6 byte-identically (A/B verified across all 7 tapes). Faithful sim change, not a regression. See docs/fidelity/ledger/FID-0117.json. Re-baselined 2026-07-12 (a12bbed88c612c91->2d4751e5b0e25cff) for FID-0123: the FP recoil-settle path now reads the named WeaponStats RecoilSpeed/RecoilBack/RecoilUp fields instead of raw N64 byte offsets (0x47/0x48/0x4C) that on the +4-shifted native struct (ptr_cartridge_struct grew 4->8B) hit high-byte-of-Sway / RecoilSpeed-as-f32(~0) / RecoilBack -> restored positional recoil kick + correct rot scale. Only weapon-firing tapes change. GE007_NO_WEAPONSTATS_RECOIL_OFFSET_FIX=1 reproduces the prior hash a12bbed88c612c91 byte-identically (A/B verified across all 7 tapes). Faithful sim change. See FID-0123. Re-baselined 2026-07-15 (default 2d4751e5b0e25cff->79483d38390b21e5, off 2c93c8d798811fe2->de395833d3531046) for FID-0131: build-environment (toolchain/link-layout) sensitivity of the Dam sim-state hash -- the hashed 8 MB pool region carries build-layout-sensitive dead/uninitialized bytes (FID-0046 class) from Dam transient file-load scratch. ON (79483d38) still != OFF (de395833), so the FID-0056/0066 fire-rate divisor is still exercised (verified: with AK47 injection the hash differs from the no-injection run). Gameplay byte-identical vs prior toolchain (trace-state unchanged). Durable zero-init BLOCKED (dead bytes span generic stage buffers -> too broad). Not a regression. NOTE: the AK47 scripted-item injection (replay_env GE007_AUTO_ADD_ITEM/EQUIP) only fires when g_CurrentPlayer!=NULL at input_frame 30; a full parallel --clean-first build was observed once to spawn the player late and MISS injection (hash collapsed to the no-weapon value d0d7501e), so if the gate ever reports d0d7501e for this tape the AK47 was not equipped -- rebuild and re-verify. See docs/fidelity/ledger/FID-0131.json. Re-baselined 2026-07-19 (79483d38390b21e5->c52be97c2c404c36) for FID-0132: the collision-hull builder sub_GAME_7F03ECC0 now stores the retail (unclamped) hull vertex count on the collision_data-backed object/door path (rect==&data->unk04, spare words unk24..unk40 are the retail overflow slots); the FID-0096 clamp-to-4 remains only on the bare-rect4f tank path. The old unconditional clamp dropped real hull corners for thin/near-axis-aligned box footprints (degenerate collision polygons -> broken point-in-polygon -> object grounding errors, e.g. the Dam control-room monitors floor-snapping 93 units below their desks; 116 spurious-degenerate footprints across dam/facility/archives/streets -> 0). Corrected footprints change object collision/grounding sim state on every object-bearing level. GE007_NO_HULL_CORNER_FIX=1 reproduces the prior hash 79483d38390b21e5 byte-identically (verified on this tape on the fixed binary). Faithful sim change, not a regression. Corpus ctest hull_builder guards the geometry. See docs/fidelity/ledger/FID-0132.json. fire_rate_authentic_off_hash likewise re-based de395833d3531046->1070b780dc571a11 (= fix-ON default + GE007_FIRE_RATE_AUTHENTIC=0, stable across 2 runs; GE007_NO_HULL_CORNER_FIX=1 + GE007_FIRE_RATE_AUTHENTIC=0 reproduces the prior off-hash de395833d3531046 byte-identically). NOTE: one opt-out replay flaked to 11ee3fd6b7419ff1 (the FID-0131 injection-race class); re-runs are stable.
